title: Level 665
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: '''''Level 665''''': '''''ℬ𝓁𝒶𝒸𝓀𝓅ℴℴ𝓁 ℬ𝓇𝒾ℊ𝒽𝓉𝓈'''''}} {{CSS|User:Lancelot_Chen/Level -500.css}} {{Background|file=Blackpool Reel.jpg}} __NOTOC__
[[File:-500 Logo.png|400px]]
'''Level 665''' is the 666th of the Backrooms.
{{Audio|file=Blackpool Imagination.mp3|volume=0.5|secrettext=refresh page|align=center}} {{Class Custom Image|text-color=blue|extra-banner-css=|bg-image=Big Dipper.png|color-h=31|safety=𝓢𝓪𝓯𝓮𝓽𝔂 𝓖𝓾𝓪𝓻𝓪𝓷𝓽𝓮𝓮𝓭|security=𝓝𝓸 𝓝𝓮𝓮𝓭 𝓯𝓸𝓻 𝓢𝓮𝓬𝓾𝓻𝓲𝓽𝔂|entity=𝓝𝓸 𝓗𝓪𝓻𝓶𝓯𝓾𝓵 𝓔𝓷𝓽𝓲𝓽𝓲𝓮𝓼|class=0|hexagon=0}} ==𝓐𝓹𝓹𝓪𝓻𝓲𝓽𝓲𝓸𝓷-𝓛𝓲𝓴𝓮 𝓢𝓽𝓪𝓽𝓮== {{Blurimage|file=Blackpool Tower.jpg|caption=The Blackpool Tower, the icon of Blackpool.|align=left}}
Level 665 is a level in which the wanderers themselves are the apparitions. Wanderers are in a ghost-like state. When wanderers look in mirrors in Level 665, They see themselves but in bright blue form. In this state, they are still like-themselves, but with heavy restrictions. For one, while they can grab candies, drinks, and other food, the "Normal People" within the level will not recognize that wanderer's actions. Like if a "Normal Person" wanted to grab the last chocolate bar that the wanderer got, but instead of the scripted candy they were supposed to get, they will grab another they like, or just not choose a candy at all. but while wanderers can have things from this level, they can't have stuff from other levels. In this state, whatever a wanderer had before in any backroom's levels, will be replaced. Instead, what one will have are items that were common in the early 1920s. Any weapons however will not be in the level and whatever weapons the wanderer had before entering the level will be permanently gone. If one had any food or water on them before entering the level, they will be replaced with branded foods and drinks from early 1927. One's attire will also change upon entering the level to that of early 1920s clothing. ==𝓣𝓱𝓮 𝓛𝓮𝓿𝓮𝓵==
Level 665 resembles the real town of Blackpool in the United Kingdom, but with a strange anomaly: the date is always June 14th, 1927. The M.E.G. theorizes that a mysterious being controls this phenomenon. Wanderers cannot leave the town because of a massive barrier visible only to them, not to the "Normal People." The level contains everything Blackpool had on June 14th, 1927 — buildings, cars, people, etc. This includes Blackpool Pleasure Beach, which features strange glitches. These glitches vary in size from a small patch of grass to an entire roller coaster. Glitches occur a few times daily and last about 30 minutes. These glitches however are not harmful at all and will just give a very mild shock if one touches a glitch. {{Blurimage|file=Blackpool Pleasure Beach Photo.png|caption=Blackpool Pleasure Beach, where joy never dies.|align=right}} A strange event occurs every night at exactly 1:37 AM in the Winter Garden's ballroom: a silent film titled '''Ballad of the Fairies''' plays. The film is set in an alternate universe where humans are replaced by fairies. It tells the story of two fairies who fall in love, growing madly in love as the film progresses. On the day of their wedding, the female fairy dies in a car accident, leaving the male devastated. The film ends there. After watching, wanderers often find themselves crying, but this crying is forced by the film. The reason is unknown, so wanderers are advised not to watch it, or only watch it once. In Stanley Park, between 2:00 AM and 2:22 AM, a bright blue floating orb appears. The orb grows brighter and then slowly fades away after two minutes. When watching the orb, wanderers experience intense memory flare-ups, both good and bad. These flare-ups continue intermittently for about an hour after the orb disappears. Wanderers are advised not to look at the orb, as it may trigger psychological trauma. If necessary, only watch it once. It is possible to communicate with people outside the level, but there is only one known method as to do so. Inside a post office controlled by the Worsley Community lies a working Telegraph key that can talk to another one outside of the level. The location of the Telegraph key outside of level 665 is unknown, though it is confirmed to be in M.E.G control. In level 665, all Worsley Community members are taught how to use the Telegraph key machine.
==𝓔𝓷𝓽𝓲𝓽𝓲𝓮𝓼==
𝓗𝓮𝓵𝓹𝓮𝓻𝓼 Helpers are friendly entities in Level 665, renowned for aiding wanderers in need. They boost morale and provide assistance whenever possible—even talking a distressed wanderer out of self-harm (one M.E.G. report credits a Helper with saving an agent’s daughter from suicide). Helpers may also trade water and food for a simple compliment. If a wanderer harbors and expresses strong negative emotions—especially anger or insults—toward a Helper, it will not retaliate, instead it will release a calming nerve gas which will calm one down to a passive state. It will then give calming words to the wanderer, and give pats on the wanderer's back. Helpers are exceedingly rare—fewer than 75 are believed to exist in this level. They can be identified by their habit of standing perfectly still, smoking a cigar or cigarette, and wearing elegant clothing
"𝓝𝓸𝓻𝓶𝓪𝓵 𝓟𝓮𝓸𝓹𝓵𝓮" “Normal People” populate the public areas of Level 665, following scripted routines and designated paths. Their routes remain the same on each reset, but once every thirty resets (about once per month), their pattern changes—sometimes as minor as choosing a different candy bar, or as major as falling ill. These path alterations do not affect wanderers. {{Blurimage|file="Normal People".jpg|caption=An photo of "Normal People" having fun at Blackpool Pleasure Beach.|align=right}} A strict rule forbids harming Normal People. Any physical or severe psychological attack triggers a personal reset: the wanderer loses memory of the past 24 hours, and the level rewinds the last few minutes to erase the incident. This reset impacts only the attacker and the Normal People involved; Helpers and other wanderers remain unaffected. Any weapon or harmful object used is removed from the attacker’s hand and replaced with flowers. Investigations have confirmed that Normal People are exact imitations of real Blackpool residents from June 14, 1927—verified by matching IDs/birth records. The mechanism behind this phenomenon is unknown, though many theorize the Overseer is responsible.
𝓣𝓱𝓮 𝓦𝓱𝓲𝓽𝓮 𝓐𝓷𝓰𝓮𝓵 The White Angel is a mysterious entity that appears only in the Winter Gardens, roughly one hour after the silent film '''Ballad of the Fairies''' ends. She takes the form of a woman in her twenties, with blonde hair, blue eyes, and white feathered wings. {{Blurimage|file=White Angel.jpg|caption=An artists conception of the White Angel.|align=right}} She greets all wanderers—except members of the Children of the Overseer, whom she quietly bars from the Winter Gardens during her hour-long visit. The reason for her disdain is unknown but is often attributed to the group’s unlawful or harmful practices. Conversations with the White Angel are limited to innocuous topics: her appearance, general advice, or simple directions within Level 665. She refuses to discuss the Overseer or the Children of the Overseer—any attempt to broach these subjects earns a polite but firm change of topic or a disapproving look. After one hour, she vanishes until the next night.
𝓣𝓱𝓮 𝓞𝓿𝓮𝓻𝓼𝓮𝓮𝓻 The Overseer is a rumored entity within Level 665. It is yet to be confirmed, but there have been strong evidence and solid eyewitness accounts of the overseer being present within this level. {{Blurimage|file=Overseer Photo.jpg|caption=Unconfirmed photograph of the overseer.|align=right}} Eyewitness accounts have said that the appearance of the overseer is that to be of a man in his 30s, wearing a black fedora hat, wearing a black shirt with a black trench coat on top. It is reported that wanderers see him in places where others should not be, observing wanderers as they walk along the level. It is most noted that most of the time, the overseer always stands in front of churches. The reason is unclear, but whenever wanderers see him in front of the churches, they have a feeling of sadness. It is also thought that the film "Ballad of the Fairies" is related to the overseer. For within the film, the appearance of the male fairy is somewhat familiar to the appearance of the overseer, with a fedora and black trench coat. Despite the film being in black and white, the trench coat is also similar in appearance to actual trench coat witnessed by wanderers in the level. The other potential link is the way how both after the film ends and when the overseer is in front of a church, it both effects the wanderer's emotions and causes them to cry. ==𝓒𝓸𝓵𝓸𝓷𝓲𝓮𝓼 𝓪𝓷𝓭 𝓞𝓾𝓽𝓹𝓸𝓼𝓽𝓼==
𝓣𝓱𝓮 𝓦𝓸𝓻𝓼𝓵𝓮𝔂 𝓒𝓸𝓶𝓶𝓾𝓷𝓲𝓽𝔂 The Worsley Community comprises about 725 residents of Level 665 who have chosen to build lives there in relative safety. They meet once a year—synchronized to real time—inside Blackpool Tower. At this gathering, new members who have arrived since the last meeting are formally introduced, and a leader is elected by a 66%-majority vote. To qualify as a candidate, an individual must have lived in the level for at least five years, hold no criminal record, and maintain good standing with the community. After introductions and the election, each new member is assigned a permanent house; until then, they reside in a communal apartment complex. A minority within the Worsley Community believe they are immortal, citing the daily reset of Level 665 as evidence that their bodies regenerate each day, preserving youth at the cost of memory. However, experiments and long-term observation have disproven this: residents age normally, and the level’s reset affects only the environment, not the individuals. Despite these findings, the myth of physical immortality persists among some members.
𝓣𝓱𝓮 𝓔𝓵𝓲𝓽𝓮𝓼 The Elites are a 25-member exclusive group drawn from the Worsley Community’s most influential residents, guiding both community policy and level-wide decisions—such as housing assignments and the timing of annual meetings. When an Elite seat is vacated by resignation or death, an emergency election is held. Eligibility requires a minimum age of 35, at least eight years’ residency, and significant contributions to the Worsley Community. In the first ballot, all qualified candidates are voted on; the top two advance to a runoff. The candidate who wins the runoff becomes the new Elite member. New Elites inherit the mansion of their predecessor, gain lifetime membership, and move into the exclusive “Victoria Community.” They also receive a luxury automobile. Thereafter, quarterly meetings in Blackpool Tower are mandatory, culminating each time in a celebratory feast hosted by the community.
𝓒𝓱𝓲𝓵𝓭𝓻𝓮𝓷 𝓸𝓯 𝓽𝓱𝓮 𝓞𝓿𝓮𝓻𝓼𝓮𝓮𝓻 The Children of the Overseer are a small cult of about 17 members devoted to the rumored Overseer. Their headquarters is in Stanley Park, where they built a statue of what they think the overseer looks like. Each member wears a blue robe and derby hat. Every day at 12:00 PM and 12:00 AM, they bow and pray to the statue, repeating the ritual every thirty minutes during those hours. At night, they watch the silent film Ballad of the Fairies, believing it honors the Overseer. After the film, they invite any wandering visitors to join them, even those who have refused before. Because the film plays only once per day, they made their own animated version. Many say it’s a good remake, though most agree the original is better. The Children also gather for the “Blue Sun” orb event in Stanley Park. When the bright blue orb appears, they bow to it and invite anyone nearby to join their group.
𝓑𝓵𝓪𝓬𝓴𝓹𝓸𝓸𝓵 𝓒𝓸𝓪𝓼𝓽𝓮𝓻 𝓔𝓷𝓽𝓱𝓾𝓼𝓲𝓪𝓽𝓼 The Blackpool Coaster Enthusiasts are a mid-sized group of about 120 members based in the Pleasure Beach area of Level 665. They’re friendly and non-hostile, often giving wanderers almond water, snacks, and other supplies. For every three bottles of almond water or food items they provide, the recipient must ride a roller coaster with them. They maintain neutral relations with both the Worsley Community (and its Elites) and the Children of the Overseer. When those groups clash, the Coaster Enthusiasts step in to mediate—even if neither side likes their help. Despite this neutrality, they quietly distrust the Children of the Overseer because of the cult’s violent behavior. Anyone who isn’t a member of the Children of the Overseer and has a good reputation in the level may join. To become a full member, a wanderer must ride every roller coaster in Blackpool Pleasure Beach. Once they’ve done that, they’re formally welcomed into the group.
==𝓔𝓷𝓽𝓻𝓪𝓷𝓬𝓮 𝓪𝓷𝓭 𝓔𝔁𝓲𝓽==
'''''The door will only show to those who truly want out of the hell that is the endless mazes and danger. If one wants only to be truly free of harm, to those who have kind hearts and just want peace, I will be your guardian angel. And as your angel, I will take you to a paradise where harm will never come to you. Where danger is nonexistent, where even others from the outside can't ever harm you again. In darkness, you will see the light where others remain blind to the darkness. In this hell, predators will always find prey. It is the very nature of which that has plagued mankind since dawn. But here, I change all of it. Instead of prey or predator, you are neither. You will not pursue nor defend. For both is not necessary. Here, everyone will have a life worth living. And it is that life, I want to share with you. So as long as you are not in any true hell where no exits are possible, my door will be open to you, and I am all but eager to meet you.''''' {{Collapse|num=1|initial='''> Exit?'''|final='''> Hide the thoughts of ever getting out of here.|body=
"...You aren't thinking of leaving aren't you? ...Of course not. After all, why would one want to leave such a wonderful paradise? Here, one can have eternal fun. From the Pleasure Beach to the Winter Gardens, there is nothing short of things to do here. You were down on your luck before you came here. Struggling to survive, barely getting by. When an opportunity knocked however, you jumped in and landed in salvation. You wouldn't have to worry anymore." "...Oh, where are my manners? I haven't introduced myself! My name is ██████ █████ ██████, but you may know me as The Overseer. You see, I had a dream. This dream was to be free, free from all dangers and worries here in all these dangerous labyrinths and landscapes. I set out to acheive this dream of mine with my beloved. She was everything to me, she was my world. ...until it happened... I vowed ever since, that no wanderers would ever be harmed by any dangers at all in this cursed realm." In the end, I managed to create this paradise of which you now live here, forever. Be that as it may, it is your destiny. You wanted salvation, and you will have it. No dangers, no worries. It is as it should be. The various lands, the various beings that inhabit it, human or not, deserved to live. Even if they want to exit, they can't as I forbid it. I can't stand to lose another soul to those pests. You will be destined to remain here, forever. There is no way out." "Do you want the truth? Why you came here? I will not go over too much, but here is my reasoning for you here. I was handed a God's hand, because I knew what it meant to truly lose someone. But it came at a price, the hand of god was but merely the offer to be a devil. They say the devil is an all-powerful force of evil. That is not true, at least here it wasn't. Here, I was the devil of not wanderers, but the entities that resided outside. But...to some wanderers I am the devil, and they made a deal with not their souls, but with entrapment. It is guaranteed they will never be harmed again, but they will never know everything else outside. This world, will be their only world." '''''}}
